languages.name: Python
languages.description: Learn Python with Corey Schafer videos, written tutorials, and practice problems.

categories.name: Corey Schafer Videos
categories.description: A curated 160-video programming track from Corey Schafer.

categories.name: Getting Started
categories.description: Install Python and write your first scripts.

categories.name: Syntax
categories.description: Read Python statements, indentation, and errors.

categories.name: Variables
categories.description: Store values with clear names and convert user input.

categories.name: Data Types
categories.description: Understand numbers, text, booleans, and None.

categories.name: Numbers
categories.description: Use arithmetic, rounding, and math helpers.

categories.name: Strings
categories.description: Work with text, slicing, formatting, and methods.

categories.name: Lists (Arrays)
categories.description: Use Python's array-like list collection.

categories.name: Tuples and Sets
categories.description: Model fixed records and unique values.

categories.name: Dictionaries
categories.description: Map keys to values for lookup and counting.

categories.name: If...Else
categories.description: Choose different paths with conditions.

categories.name: Loops
categories.description: Repeat work with for loops and while loops.

categories.name: Functions
categories.description: Package logic into testable reusable blocks.

categories.name: Classes and Objects
categories.description: Model data and behavior with objects.

categories.name: Files and Modules
categories.description: Read files and split code across modules.

categories.name: Exceptions
categories.description: Handle expected failures clearly.

categories.name: JSON
categories.description: Read and write structured JSON data.

categories.name: Date and Time
categories.description: Use dates, times, and formatting.

categories.name: Testing
categories.description: Check code with asserts and unit tests.

categories.name: Problem Solving
categories.description: Turn coding prompts into small steps.

lessons.title: What Python is used for
lessons.description: See where Python fits in apps, scripts, data, web backends, and automation.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Python is a general purpose language with a small readable syntax. In PyQuest, use it first for problem solving and small console programs.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
List three tasks you could automate with Python.

lessons.title: Install Python and run a file
lessons.description: Install Python, check the version, and run a .py file from a terminal.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use python --version to confirm the install. Create one small file, save it, then run python filename.py.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Create hello.py and print your name.

lessons.title: Your first Python program
lessons.description: Use print, comments, and simple expressions to understand output.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A program runs from top to bottom. print() sends text to the console. Comments start with # and explain intent.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print three lines about yourself.

lessons.title: Input and output
lessons.description: Read text with input() and display results with print().
lessons.content:
Key idea
input() always returns a string. Convert it with int() or float() before arithmetic.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Read a name and age, then print a greeting.

lessons.title: Indentation
lessons.description: Use indentation to show which lines belong together.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Python uses indentation instead of braces. Keep one consistent indent level, usually four spaces.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Fix an if statement with incorrect indentation.

lessons.title: Statements and expressions
lessons.description: Understand the difference between instructions and values.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A statement does work. An expression produces a value. Many beginner bugs come from using one where the other is expected.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Predict the value of 2 + 3 * 4.

lessons.title: Comments
lessons.description: Write short comments that explain why code exists.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Comments start with #. Use them for intent, edge cases, and reminders, not for repeating obvious code.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Add helpful comments to a small calculator.

lessons.title: Common syntax errors
lessons.description: Recognize missing colons, quotes, parentheses, and indentation mistakes.
lessons.content:
Key idea
When code fails before running, read the line number and also inspect the line just above it.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Fix three broken snippets by reading their error messages.

lessons.title: Variables and assignment
lessons.description: Store values with meaningful names.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A variable points to a value. Assignment uses =. Comparison uses ==.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Calculate a rectangle area from width and height.

lessons.title: Naming style
lessons.description: Choose names that make code easy to scan.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use lowercase_with_underscores for variables and functions. Prefer total_score over x when meaning matters.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Rename unclear variables in a small program.

lessons.title: Casting input
lessons.description: Convert strings into numbers or other types when needed.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use int(), float(), str(), and bool() intentionally. Convert near the input boundary so later code receives the right type.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Read two numbers as strings and print their sum.

lessons.title: Check data types
lessons.description: Use type() and isinstance() while debugging.
lessons.content:
Key idea
type(value) shows the exact type. isinstance(value, int) is better when checking categories of values.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print the type of five different values.

lessons.title: Booleans
lessons.description: Represent true or false conditions.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Comparisons such as age >= 18 return booleans. Combine them with and, or, and not.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Check whether a score is between 0 and 100.

lessons.title: None
lessons.description: Represent an intentionally missing value.
lessons.content:
Key idea
None is not 0, an empty string, or False. Use is None when checking for missing values.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Return None when a search does not find a match.

lessons.title: Integers and floats
lessons.description: Use whole numbers and decimal numbers correctly.
lessons.content:
Key idea
int is exact for whole numbers. float is approximate for decimal calculations.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Read two prices and print the total.

lessons.title: Math operators
lessons.content:
Key idea
// gives floor division. % gives the remainder. ** calculates powers.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print quotient, remainder, and square of a number.

lessons.title: Rounding numbers
lessons.description: Round values for display without changing core logic too early.
lessons.content:
Key idea
round(value, 2) is useful for display. Keep exact values while calculating, then format at the edge.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print an average rounded to two decimals.

lessons.title: String basics
lessons.description: Create, combine, and measure text.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Strings are sequences of characters. Use len() for length and + or f-strings to combine text.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print the length of a username.

lessons.title: Indexing and slicing
lessons.description: Read parts of a string with indexes and ranges.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Indexes start at 0. Slices use start:stop and do not include the stop index.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print the first, last, and reversed version of a word.

lessons.title: f-strings
lessons.description: Format variables into readable output.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Put f before the string and write variables inside braces. This keeps output clear.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print 'Alice scored 95 points' from variables.

lessons.title: String methods
lessons.description: Use built-in helpers like strip, lower, split, and replace.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Methods return new strings because strings are immutable. Assign the result if you need to keep it.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Normalize an email by trimming spaces and lowercasing it.

lessons.title: List basics
lessons.description: Store ordered, changeable data.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A Python list is the everyday array-like container. Use append(), indexing, slicing, and loops.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Store five scores and print the highest.

lessons.title: List methods
lessons.description: Add, remove, sort, and count items.
lessons.content:
Key idea
append adds one item, extend adds many, remove deletes by value, and pop removes by position.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Remove negative numbers from a list.

lessons.title: List comprehensions
lessons.description: Build lists from existing data compactly.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A comprehension is a loop expression. Use it when it stays readable.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Create a list of squares from 1 to 10.

lessons.title: Nested lists
lessons.description: Represent grids and tables.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A nested list is a list of lists. Access values with two indexes, such as grid[row][col].

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print every value in a 3 by 3 grid.

lessons.title: Tuples
lessons.description: Represent fixed groups of values.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Tuples are immutable. Use them for coordinates, pairs, and multiple return values.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Return both min and max from a function.

lessons.title: Unpacking
lessons.description: Assign multiple values from a tuple or list.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Unpacking makes structured values readable. The number of variables must match the values.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Unpack a name and score from a pair.

lessons.title: Sets
lessons.description: Keep unique values and compare groups.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Sets remove duplicates and support union, intersection, and difference.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Find common items between two lists.

lessons.title: Dictionary basics
lessons.description: Map keys to values.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Dictionaries are ideal for records, lookup tables, and counting. Keys should be stable values such as strings or numbers.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Create a profile dictionary with name and age.

lessons.title: Loop through dictionaries
lessons.description: Read keys and values clearly.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use items() when you need both key and value. Use get() when a key may be missing.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print each product name and price.

lessons.title: Counting with dictionaries
lessons.description: Count frequency of values.
lessons.content:
Key idea
For each item, update a count. This pattern appears often in coding interviews and data cleanup.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Count words in a sentence.

lessons.title: if, elif, and else
lessons.description: Choose a branch based on conditions.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use if for the first condition, elif for alternatives, and else as the fallback.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Classify a score as fail, pass, or excellent.

lessons.title: Comparison chains
lessons.description: Write readable range checks.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Python allows 0 <= score <= 100. This is clearer than two separate comparisons.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Check whether a temperature is in a safe range.

lessons.title: match and case
lessons.description: Choose behavior from known patterns.
lessons.content:
Key idea
match is useful for command-style values. Keep cases simple when learning.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Handle commands start, stop, and help.

lessons.title: for loops
lessons.description: Repeat work over ranges and collections.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A for loop visits each item in a sequence. range(n) gives 0 through n - 1.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Sum all even numbers from 1 to n.

lessons.title: while loops
lessons.description: Repeat while a condition remains true.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A while loop is useful when you do not know the number of repeats in advance.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Keep asking for input until the user types quit.

lessons.title: break and continue
lessons.description: Control loop flow.
lessons.content:
Key idea
break exits the loop. continue jumps to the next iteration. Use them when they simplify the loop.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Find the first number divisible by 7.

lessons.title: Define functions
lessons.description: Create named blocks of reusable logic.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A function has a name, parameters, and a body. return sends a value back to the caller.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Write a function that returns the larger of two numbers.

lessons.title: Parameters and defaults
lessons.description: Pass data into functions safely.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Parameters are names inside the function. Defaults are useful when one value is common.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Write a greeting function with a default language.

lessons.title: Return values
lessons.description: Design functions that are easy to test.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Returning is different from printing. A returned value can be reused by other code.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Return a list of squares from a list of numbers.

lessons.title: Scope
lessons.description: Understand local and global names.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Variables created inside a function are local. Prefer returning values instead of changing globals.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Fix a function that accidentally depends on a global variable.

lessons.title: Classes and objects
lessons.description: Group data and behavior into a custom type.
lessons.content:
Key idea
A class defines the shape. An object is an instance. __init__ sets starting attributes.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Create a Student class with name and score.

lessons.title: Methods and self
lessons.description: Attach behavior to objects.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Methods are functions inside classes. self refers to the current object.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Add a passed() method to Student.

lessons.title: Inheritance basics
lessons.description: Share behavior between related classes.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Inheritance can remove duplication, but use it only when objects truly have an is-a relationship.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Create Animal and Dog classes.

lessons.title: Useful dunder methods
lessons.description: Customize string display and equality.
lessons.content:
Key idea
__str__ makes objects readable when printed. __repr__ helps debugging.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Implement __str__ for a Book class.

lessons.title: Read files
lessons.description: Use with open(...) to read text safely.
lessons.content:
Key idea
The with block closes files automatically. Read small files with read(), or loop line by line.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Read all lines from notes.txt.

lessons.title: Write files
lessons.description: Save text output from a program.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use mode 'w' to overwrite and 'a' to append. Be careful with paths when running from different folders.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Write a list of names to a file.

lessons.title: Imports and modules
lessons.description: Split code across files and use standard library modules.
lessons.content:
Key idea
import gives access to another module. Keep imports at the top so dependencies are visible.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Use math.sqrt and random.randint in a script.

lessons.title: Paths
lessons.description: Work with file and folder paths.
lessons.content:
Key idea
pathlib.Path gives readable path operations across operating systems.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Build a path to a data file beside your script.

lessons.title: try and except
lessons.description: Handle expected failures without crashing.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Put risky code in try and recovery logic in except. Catch specific exceptions when possible.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Keep asking for a number until parsing succeeds.

lessons.title: else and finally
lessons.description: Run cleanup or success-only logic.
lessons.content:
Key idea
else runs when no exception occurs. finally runs whether an exception occurred or not.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print a cleanup message after reading a file.

lessons.title: JSON strings
lessons.description: Convert between JSON text and Python data.
lessons.content:
Key idea
json.loads parses a string. json.dumps creates a JSON string.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Turn a profile dictionary into JSON text.

lessons.title: JSON files
lessons.description: Save and load structured data files.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Use json.dump for files and json.load to read them back.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Save app settings to settings.json.

lessons.title: datetime basics
lessons.description: Represent dates and times.
lessons.content:
Key idea
The datetime module gives date, time, datetime, and timedelta types.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Print today's date.

lessons.title: Format dates
lessons.description: Convert dates to and from readable strings.
lessons.content:
Key idea
strftime formats a datetime. strptime parses a string into a datetime.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Parse '2026-06-24' into a date.

lessons.title: Assertions
lessons.description: Check assumptions while learning.
lessons.content:
Key idea
assert stops the program if a condition is false. It is useful for tiny practice checks.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Assert that add(2, 3) equals 5.

lessons.title: Unit tests
lessons.description: Test functions in repeatable files.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Unit tests call functions with known inputs and compare the output to expected values.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Write two tests for a max_of_two function.

lessons.title: Read the prompt
lessons.description: Turn a coding prompt into inputs, outputs, and constraints.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Before coding, identify what data comes in, what must go out, and which edge cases matter.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Annotate a two-sum style prompt.

lessons.title: Solve in small steps
lessons.description: Break a problem into helper decisions.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Write a simple version first, then improve. Print intermediate values while learning.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Solve a list sum problem before adding validation.

lessons.title: Debugging practice
lessons.description: Use tracebacks and small examples to find bugs.
lessons.content:
Key idea
Debugging is narrowing the problem. Reproduce it, inspect values, change one thing, and run again.

Beginner checklist
- Identify the input, output, and important variables.
- Run one tiny example before the full solution.
- Read errors from the first useful line, then fix one thing at a time.

Practice
Fix an off-by-one loop bug.
